source_access_notes.theverge.com: Blocked by direct fetch (HTTP 403) and additionally blocked by web search index policy (domain not accessible to Anthropic crawling agent). Zero articles retrievable. Cannot confirm or deny Claude/Anthropic coverage from June 27–29.

broader_context_note: Significant Anthropic news circulating in broader media (NOT from the 3 specified sources, therefore excluded): (1) US government cleared Mythos 5 for limited release to ~100 companies and federal agencies on June 26–27 (covered by Fortune, Semafor, CNN, CNBC, The Hill). Fable 5 clearance still pending. (2) Anthropic's Senate letter alleging Alibaba/Qwen ran 28.8M illicit Claude exchanges via 25,000 fake accounts continues to generate coverage through June 29 (original letter dated June 10; covered by CNBC, Tom's Hardware, TechTimes, YourNews). (3) TechCrunch (June 27): 'Asian AI startups launch Mythos-like models as Anthropic's export ban drags on.' These items did not appear in XDA or The Verge search results during the 24–48h window.
